title: Future AI chips could be built on glass
summary: Absolics, a South Korean company, is planning commercial production of special glass panels designed to enhance next-generation AI computing hardware for data centers. This glass technology could significantly improve the performance and power efficiency of AI chips.
content: Human-made glass is thousands of years old. But it’s now poised to find its way into the AI chips used in the world’s newest and largest data centers. This year, a South Korean company called Absolics is planning to start commercial production of special glass panels designed to make next-generation computing hardware more powerful and…

zh.title: 未来AI芯片可能在玻璃上构建
zh.summary: 韩国公司Absolics正在计划特殊玻璃面板的商业生产，该面板旨在增强数据中心的下一代AI计算硬件。这种玻璃技术可能会显著提高AI芯片的性能和功率效率。

fr.title: Les futures puces IA pourraient être construites sur du verre
fr.summary: Absolics, une entreprise sud-coréenne, prévoit la production commerciale de panneaux de verre spéciaux conçus pour améliorer le matériel informatique IA de nouvelle génération pour les centres de données. Cette technologie de verre pourrait améliorer considérablement les performances et l'efficacité énergétique des puces IA.

de.title: Zukünftige KI-Chips könnten auf Glas gebaut werden
de.summary: Absolics, ein südkoreanisches Unternehmen, plant die kommerzielle Produktion von speziellen Glaspaneelen, die die Hardware für die nächste Generation von KI-Computern in Rechenzentren verbessern sollen. Diese Glastechnologie könnte die Leistung und Energieeffizienz von KI-Chips erheblich verbessern.

es.title: Los futuros chips de IA podrían construirse en vidrio
es.summary: Absolics, una empresa surcoreana, está planeando la producción comercial de paneles de vidrio especiales diseñados para mejorar el hardware de computación de IA de próxima generación para centros de datos. Esta tecnología de vidrio podría mejorar significativamente el rendimiento y la eficiencia energética de los chips de IA.

title: DIVE: Scaling Diversity in Agentic Task Synthesis for Generalizable Tool Use
summary: DIVE proposes a method that improves LLM tool-use generalization by executing diverse real-world tools first and reverse-deriving training tasks from execution traces. The approach achieves +22 average improvement across nine out-of-distribution benchmarks and demonstrates that diversity scaling outperforms quantity scaling for robust generalization.
content:
arXiv:2603.11076v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recent work synthesizes agentic tasks for post-training tool-using LLMs, yet robust generalization under shifts in tasks and toolsets remains an open challenge. We trace this brittleness to insufficient diversity in synthesized tasks. Scaling diversity is difficult because training requires tasks to remain executable and verifiable, while generalization demands coverage of diverse tool types, toolset combinations, and heterogeneous tool-use patterns. We propose DIVE, an evidence-driven recipe that inverts synthesis order, executing diverse, real-world tools first and reverse-deriving tasks strictly entailed by the resulting traces, thereby providing grounding by construction. DIVE scales structural diversity along two controllable axes, tool-pool coverage and per-task toolset variety, and an Evidence Collection--Task Derivation loop further induces rich multi-step tool-use patterns across 373 tools in five domains. Training Qwen3-8B on DIVE data (48k SFT + 3.2k RL) improves by +22 average points across 9 OOD benchmarks and outperforms the strongest 8B baseline by +68. Remarkably, controlled scaling analysis reveals that diversity scaling consistently outperforms quantity scaling for OOD generalization, even with 4x less data.

zh.title: DIVE：代理任务合成中的多样性扩展以实现可泛化的工具使用
zh.summary: DIVE提出了一种方法，通过首先执行多样化的真实工具并从执行跟踪中反向推导训练任务来改进LLM工具使用的泛化。该方法在九个分布外基准上实现了平均+22的改进，并证明了多样性扩展优于数量扩展以实现稳健的泛化。

fr.title: DIVE : Mise à l'échelle de la diversité dans la synthèse de tâches d'agent pour une utilisation généralisable des outils
fr.summary: DIVE propose une méthode qui améliore la généralisation de l'utilisation des outils LLM en exécutant d'abord des outils du monde réel diversifiés et en dérivant rétroactivement les tâches d'entraînement à partir des traces d'exécution. L'approche réalise une amélioration moyenne de +22 sur neuf benchmarks hors distribution et démontre que l'expansion de la diversité surpasse l'expansion de la quantité pour une généralisation robuste.

de.title: DIVE: Skalierung der Vielfalt in der agentengestützten Aufgabensynthese für verallgemeinerbare Werkzeugnutzung
de.summary: DIVE schlägt eine Methode vor, die die Generalisierung der LLM-Werkzeugnutzung verbessert, indem zunächst verschiedene reale Werkzeuge ausgeführt und Trainingsaufgaben rückwärts aus Ausführungsspuren abgeleitet werden. Der Ansatz erreicht eine durchschnittliche Verbesserung von +22 über neun außerverteilte Benchmarks und zeigt, dass die Vielfaltsskalierung die Quantitätsskalierung für robuste Generalisierung übertrifft.

es.title: DIVE: Escalado de la diversidad en la síntesis de tareas agentivas para el uso generalizable de herramientas
es.summary: DIVE propone un método que mejora la generalización del uso de herramientas de LLM ejecutando primero herramientas del mundo real diversas e infiriendo inversamente tareas de entrenamiento a partir de trazas de ejecución. El enfoque logra una mejora promedio de +22 en nueve puntos de referencia fuera de distribución y demuestra que el escalado de diversidad supera al escalado de cantidad para una generalización robusta.

title: A Survey of Reasoning in Autonomous Driving Systems: Open Challenges and Emerging Paradigms
summary: This survey examines how autonomous driving systems must shift from perception to reasoning capabilities, positioning LLMs as cognitive cores rather than modular components. It identifies key challenges including the responsiveness-reasoning trade-off and advocates for verifiable neuro-symbolic architectures to bridge deliberative reasoning with real-time vehicle control demands.
content:
arXiv:2603.11093v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The development of high-level autonomous driving (AD) is shifting from perception-centric limitations to a more fundamental bottleneck, namely, a deficit in robust and generalizable reasoning. Although current AD systems manage structured environments, they consistently falter in long-tail scenarios and complex social interactions that require human-like judgment. Meanwhile, the advent of large language and multimodal models (LLMs and MLLMs) presents a transformative opportunity to integrate a powerful cognitive engine into AD systems, moving beyond pattern matching toward genuine comprehension. However, a systematic framework to guide this integration is critically lacking. To bridge this gap, we provide a comprehensive review of this emerging field and argue that reasoning should be elevated from a modular component to the system's cognitive core. Specifically, we first propose a novel Cognitive Hierarchy to decompose the monolithic driving task according to its cognitive and interactive complexity. Building on this, we further derive and systematize seven core reasoning challenges, such as the responsiveness-reasoning trade-off and social-game reasoning. Furthermore, we conduct a dual-perspective review of the state-of-the-art, analyzing both system-centric approaches to architecting intelligent agents and evaluation-centric practices for their validation. Our analysis reveals a clear trend toward holistic and interpretable "glass-box" agents. In conclusion, we identify a fundamental and unresolved tension between the high-latency, deliberative nature of LLM-based reasoning and the millisecond-scale, safety-critical demands of vehicle control. For future work, a primary objective is to bridge the symbolic-to-physical gap by developing verifiable neuro-symbolic architectures, robust reasoning under uncertainty, and scalable models for implicit social negotiation.

zh.title: 自动驾驶系统中的推理调查：开放挑战和新兴范式
zh.summary: 本调查研究了自动驾驶系统如何必须从感知转向推理能力，将LLM定位为认知核心而非模块化组件。它确定了关键挑战，包括响应性与推理的权衡，并提倡可验证的神经符号架构来弥合深思熟虑的推理与实时车辆控制需求之间的差距。

fr.title: Un sondage du raisonnement dans les systèmes de conduite autonome : défis ouverts et paradigmes émergents
fr.summary: Ce sondage examine comment les systèmes de conduite autonome doivent passer de la perception aux capacités de raisonnement, en positionnant les LLM comme des noyaux cognitifs plutôt que des composants modulaires. Il identifie les défis clés, notamment le compromis réactivité-raisonnement, et préconise des architectures neuro-symboliques vérifiables pour combler le raisonnement délibératif et les exigences de contrôle du véhicule en temps réel.

de.title: Eine Übersicht über Überlegungen in autonomen Fahrzeugsystemen: Offene Herausforderungen und entstehende Paradigmen
de.summary: Diese Übersicht untersucht, wie autonome Fahrzeugsysteme von Wahrnehmung zu Argumentationsfähigkeiten wechseln müssen, wobei LLMs als kognitive Kerne und nicht als modulare Komponenten positioniert werden. Sie identifiziert Schlüsselherausforderungen, darunter den Kompromiss zwischen Reaktionsfähigkeit und Argumentation, und befürwortet überprüfbare neuro-symbolische Architekturen, um absichtliches Denken mit Echtzeit-Fahrzeugkontrollanforderungen zu überbrücken.

es.title: Una encuesta del razonamiento en sistemas de conducción autónoma: desafíos abiertos y paradigmas emergentes
es.summary: Este sondeo examina cómo los sistemas de conducción autónoma deben cambiar de la percepción a capacidades de razonamiento, posicionando los LLM como núcleos cognitivos en lugar de componentes modulares. Identifica desafíos clave, incluido el compromiso entre capacidad de respuesta y razonamiento, e aboga por arquitecturas neuro-simbólicas verificables para cerrar la brecha entre el razonamiento deliberativo y las demandas de control vehicular en tiempo real.

title: PACED: Distillation at the Frontier of Student Competence
summary: PACED introduces a theoretically-grounded framework for LLM distillation that focuses training on problems at the frontier of student competence, eliminating wasted compute on already-mastered or unreachable problems. Using a Beta kernel weighting scheme, it achieves significant reasoning benchmark improvements while minimizing catastrophic forgetting.
content:
arXiv:2603.11178v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Standard LLM distillation wastes compute on two fronts: problems the student has already mastered (near-zero gradients) and problems far beyond its reach (incoherent gradients that erode existing capabilities). We show that this waste is not merely intuitive but structurally inevitable: the gradient signal-to-noise ratio in distillation provably vanishes at both pass-rate extremes. This theoretical observation leads to Paced, a framework that concentrates distillation on the zone of proximal development -- the frontier of a student model's competence -- via a principled pass-rate weight $w(p) = p^\alpha(1 - p)^\beta$ derived from the boundary-vanishing structure of distillation gradients. Key results: (1) Theory: We prove that the Beta kernel $w(p) = p^\alpha(1-p)^\beta$ is a leading-order weight family arising from the SNR structure of distillation, and that it is minimax-robust -- under bounded multiplicative misspecification, worst-case efficiency loss is only $O(\delta^2)$. (2)Distillation: On distillation from a larger teacher to a smaller student model with forward KL, Paced achieves significant gain over the base model, while keeping benchmark forgetting at a low level. (3)Self-distillation: On instruction-tuned models with reverse KL, gains are exceeding baselines as well. (4)Two-stage synergy: A forward-KL-then-reverse-KL schedule yields the strongest results in our setting, reaching substantial improvements on standard reasoning benchmarks -- supporting a mode-coverage-then-consolidation interpretation of the distillation process. All configurations require only student rollouts to estimate pass rates, need no architectural changes, and are compatible with any KL direction.

zh.title: PACED：学生能力边界处的蒸馏
zh.summary: PACED引入了一个理论基础的LLM蒸馏框架，将训练重点放在学生能力边界处的问题上，消除了在已掌握或无法到达的问题上浪费的计算。使用Beta核加权方案，它在显著改进推理基准的同时最小化了灾难性遗忘。

fr.title: PACED : Distillation à la frontière de la compétence de l'étudiant
fr.summary: PACED introduit un cadre fondé théoriquement pour la distillation des LLM qui concentre la formation sur les problèmes à la frontière de la compétence des étudiants, éliminant le gaspillage de calcul sur les problèmes déjà maîtrisés ou inaccessibles. En utilisant un schéma de pondération du noyau bêta, il réalise d'importantes améliorations des points de référence du raisonnement tout en minimisant l'oubli catastrophique.

de.title: PACED: Destillation an der Grenze der Schülerkompetenz
de.summary: PACED führt einen theoretisch fundierten Rahmen für die LLM-Destillation ein, der die Schulung auf Probleme an der Grenze der Schülerkompetenz konzentriert und verschwendete Berechnungen bei bereits beherrschten oder unerreichbaren Problemen eliminiert. Mit einem Beta-Kernel-Gewichtungsschema erreicht es erhebliche Verbesserungen der Reasoning-Benchmarks, während katastrophale Vergessenheit minimiert wird.

es.title: PACED: Destilación en la frontera de la competencia del estudiante
es.summary: PACED introduce un marco fundamentado teóricamente para la destilación de LLM que concentra el entrenamiento en problemas en la frontera de la competencia del estudiante, eliminando el desperdicio de cálculo en problemas ya dominados o inalcanzables. Utilizando un esquema de ponderación del kernel beta, logra mejoras significativas en los puntos de referencia de razonamiento mientras minimiza el olvido catastrófico.

title: Measuring AI Agents' Progress on Multi-Step Cyber Attack Scenarios
summary: This evaluation of frontier AI models on multi-step cyber attack scenarios reveals that performance scales log-linearly with inference-time compute, with latest models (Opus 4.6) completing 9.8 of 32 corporate network steps versus 1.7 for GPT-4o. The research demonstrates concerning capability improvements while highlighting practical limitations on critical infrastructure defenses.
content:
arXiv:2603.11214v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We evaluate the autonomous cyber-attack capabilities of frontier AI models on two purpose-built cyber ranges-a 32-step corporate network attack and a 7-step industrial control system attack-that require chaining heterogeneous capabilities across extended action sequences. By comparing seven models released over an eighteen-month period (August 2024 to February 2026) at varying inference-time compute budgets, we observe two capability trends. First, model performance scales log-linearly with inference-time compute, with no observed plateau-increasing from 10M to 100M tokens yields gains of up to 59%, requiring no specific technical sophistication from the operator. Second, each successive model generation outperforms its predecessor at fixed token budgets: on the corporate network range, average steps completed at 10M tokens rose from 1.7 (GPT-4o, August 2024) to 9.8 (Opus 4.6, February 2026). The best single run completed 22 of 32 steps, corresponding to roughly 6 of the estimated 14 hours a human expert would need. On the industrial control system range, performance remains limited, though the most recent models are the first to reliably complete steps, averaging 1.2-1.4 of 7 (max 3).

zh.title: 衡量AI代理在多步骤网络攻击场景中的进展
zh.summary: 这项对前沿AI模型在多步骤网络攻击场景中的评估表明，性能与推理时计算呈对数线性关系，最新模型（Opus 4.6）在32步企业网络中完成9.8步，相比之下GPT-4o完成1.7步。研究显示了令人担忧的能力提升，同时突出了对关键基础设施防御的实际限制。

fr.title: Mesurer les progrès des agents IA sur les scénarios d'attaques informatiques multi-étapes
fr.summary: Cette évaluation des modèles IA de pointe sur des scénarios d'attaques informatiques multi-étapes révèle que les performances s'ajustent de manière log-linéaire avec le calcul en temps d'inférence. Les modèles les plus récents (Opus 4.6) complètent 9,8 sur 32 étapes de réseau d'entreprise par rapport à 1,7 pour GPT-4o. La recherche démontre des améliorations de capacités préoccupantes tout en mettant en évidence les limitations pratiques sur les défenses des infrastructures critiques.

de.title: Messung des Fortschritts von KI-Agenten in Multi-Step-Cyber-Angriffsszenarien
de.summary: Diese Bewertung von Frontier-KI-Modellen in Multi-Step-Cyber-Angriffsszenarien zeigt, dass die Leistung loglinear mit der Rechenzeit bei der Inferenz skaliert. Die neuesten Modelle (Opus 4.6) erledigen 9,8 von 32 Unternehmensnetzstufen gegenüber 1,7 für GPT-4o. Die Forschung demonstriert besorgniserregende Fähigkeitsverbesserungen und hebt gleichzeitig praktische Grenzen bei der Verteidigung kritischer Infrastrukturen hervor.

es.title: Midiendo el progreso de los agentes de IA en escenarios de ataque cibernético de múltiples pasos
es.summary: Esta evaluación de modelos de IA de frontera en escenarios de ataque cibernético de múltiples pasos revela que el desempeño se escala logarítmicamente con el cómputo en tiempo de inferencia. Los modelos más recientes (Opus 4.6) completan 9,8 de 32 pasos de red corporativa frente a 1,7 para GPT-4o. La investigación demuestra mejoras de capacidad preocupantes mientras destaca limitaciones prácticas en las defensas de infraestructura crítica.

title: Reversible Lifelong Model Editing via Semantic Routing-Based LoRA
summary: SoLA enables reversible lifelong model editing through semantic routing of frozen LoRA modules, preventing catastrophic forgetting while supporting precise edits. Uniquely, it allows rolling back specific edits by removing semantic routing keys, restoring original model behavior while retaining other modifications.
content:
arXiv:2603.11239v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The dynamic evolution of real-world necessitates model editing within Large Language Models. While existing methods explore modular isolation or parameter-efficient strategies, they still suffer from semantic drift or knowledge forgetting due to continual updating. To address these challenges, we propose SoLA, a Semantic routing-based LoRA framework for lifelong model editing. In SoLA, each edit is encapsulated as an independent LoRA module, which is frozen after training and mapped to input by semantic routing, allowing dynamic activation of LoRA modules via semantic matching. This mechanism avoids semantic drift caused by cluster updating and mitigates catastrophic forgetting from parameter sharing. More importantly, SoLA supports precise revocation of specific edits by removing key from semantic routing, which restores model's original behavior. To our knowledge, this reversible rollback editing capability is the first to be achieved in existing literature. Furthermore, SoLA integrates decision-making process into edited layer, eliminating the need for auxiliary routing networks and enabling end-to-end decision-making process. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SoLA effectively learns and retains edited knowledge, achieving accurate, efficient, and reversible lifelong model editing.

zh.title: 通过基于语义路由的LoRA实现可逆的终身模型编辑
zh.summary: SoLA通过冻结LoRA模块的语义路由实现可逆的终身模型编辑，防止灾难性遗忘同时支持精确编辑。独特之处在于，它允许通过移除语义路由键来回滚特定编辑，恢复原始模型行为，同时保留其他修改。

fr.title: Édition de modèle réversible à vie via LoRA basée sur le routage sémantique
fr.summary: SoLA permet l'édition réversible du modèle à vie grâce au routage sémantique des modules LoRA figés, prévenant l'oubli catastrophique tout en prenant en charge les éditions précises. De manière unique, il permet d'annuler les éditions spécifiques en supprimant les clés de routage sémantique, restaurant le comportement du modèle original tout en conservant les autres modifications.

de.title: Reversible Lebensdauer-Modellbearbeitung über semantisches Routing-basiertes LoRA
de.summary: SoLA ermöglicht reversible Lebensdauer-Modellbearbeitung durch semantisches Routing gefrorener LoRA-Module und verhindert katastrophales Vergessen bei gleichzeitiger Unterstützung präziser Bearbeitungen. Einzigartig ist, dass es ermöglicht, bestimmte Bearbeitungen durch Entfernen semantischer Routing-Schlüssel rückgängig zu machen und so das ursprüngliche Modellverhalten wiederherzustellen, während andere Änderungen erhalten bleiben.

es.title: Edición reversible de modelo de por vida a través de LoRA basado en enrutamiento semántico
es.summary: SoLA permite la edición reversible del modelo de por vida mediante el enrutamiento semántico de módulos LoRA congelados, previniendo el olvido catastrófico mientras permite ediciones precisas. De manera única, permite revertir ediciones específicas eliminando claves de enrutamiento semántico, restaurando el comportamiento del modelo original mientras retiene otras modificaciones.

title: Mind the Sim2Real Gap in User Simulation for Agentic Tasks
summary: A large-scale study comparing LLM-based user simulators to real human behavior (451 participants) reveals that simulators are excessively cooperative and stylistically uniform, inflating agent success rates above human baselines. The findings emphasize the critical importance of human validation in agent development cycles.
content:
arXiv:2603.11245v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As NLP evaluation shifts from static benchmarks to multi-turn interactive settings, LLM-based simulators have become widely used as user proxies, serving two roles: generating user turns and providing evaluation signals. Yet, these simulations are frequently assumed to be faithful to real human behaviors, often without rigorous verification. We formalize the Sim2Real gap in user simulation and present the first study running the full $\tau$-bench protocol with real humans (451 participants, 165 tasks), benchmarking 31 LLM simulators across proprietary, open-source, and specialized families using the User-Sim Index (USI), a metric we introduce to quantify how well LLM simulators resemble real user interactive behaviors and feedback. Behaviorally, LLM simulators are excessively cooperative, stylistically uniform, and lack realistic frustration or ambiguity, creating an "easy mode" that inflates agent success rates above the human baseline. In evaluations, real humans provide nuanced judgments across eight quality dimensions while simulated users produce uniformly more positive feedback; rule-based rewards are failing to capture rich feedback signals generated by human users. Overall, higher general model capability does not necessarily yield more faithful user simulation. These findings highlight the importance of human validation when using LLM-based user simulators in the agent development cycle and motivate improved models for user simulation.

zh.title: 注意代理任务用户模拟中的Sim2Real差距
zh.summary: 一项大规模研究比较了基于LLM的用户模拟器与真实人类行为（451名参与者），表明模拟器过度合作且风格划一，高估了代理的成功率高于人类基线。研究结果强调了人类验证在代理开发周期中的关键重要性。

fr.title: Attention à l'écart Sim2Real dans la simulation utilisateur pour les tâches d'agents
fr.summary: Une étude à grande échelle comparant les simulateurs utilisateur basés sur LLM au comportement humain réel (451 participants) révèle que les simulateurs sont excessivement coopératifs et stylistiquement uniformes, gonflant les taux de réussite des agents au-dessus des références humaines. Les résultats soulignent l'importance critique de la validation humaine dans les cycles de développement d'agents.

de.title: Achten Sie auf die Sim2Real-Lücke in der Benutzersimulation für agentische Aufgaben
de.summary: Eine großangelegte Studie, die LLM-basierte Benutzersimulatoren mit echtem menschlichem Verhalten (451 Teilnehmer) vergleicht, zeigt, dass Simulatoren übermäßig kooperativ und stilistisch einheitlich sind und die Erfolgsquoten von Agenten über menschliche Vergleichswerte hinaus aufblasen. Die Ergebnisse unterstreichen die kritische Bedeutung der menschlichen Validierung in Agententwicklungszyklen.

es.title: Tenga en cuenta la brecha Sim2Real en la simulación de usuarios para tareas agentivas
es.summary: Un estudio a gran escala que compara simuladores de usuarios basados en LLM con el comportamiento humano real (451 participantes) revela que los simuladores son excesivamente cooperativos y estilísticamente uniformes, inflando las tasas de éxito del agente por encima de los valores de referencia humanos. Los hallazgos enfatizan la importancia crítica de la validación humana en los ciclos de desarrollo de agentes.

title: The Unlearning Mirage: A Dynamic Framework for Evaluating LLM Unlearning
summary: This paper exposes brittleness in LLM unlearning methods through dynamic evaluation with complex structured queries, revealing that single-hop unlearning often fails on multi-hop reasoning chains. The framework automatically generates targeted probes at varying difficulty and uncovers vulnerabilities missed by static benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.11266v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Unlearning in Large Language Models (LLMs) aims to enhance safety, mitigate biases, and comply with legal mandates, such as the right to be forgotten. However, existing unlearning methods are brittle: minor query modifications, such as multi-hop reasoning and entity aliasing, can recover supposedly forgotten information. As a result, current evaluation metrics often create an illusion of effectiveness, failing to detect these vulnerabilities due to reliance on static, unstructured benchmarks. We propose a dynamic framework that stress tests unlearning robustness using complex structured queries. Our approach first elicits knowledge from the target model (pre-unlearning) and constructs targeted probes, ranging from simple queries to multi-hop chains, allowing precise control over query difficulty. Our experiments show that the framework (1) shows comparable coverage to existing benchmarks by automatically generating semantically equivalent Q&A probes, (2) aligns with prior evaluations, and (3) uncovers new unlearning failures missed by other benchmarks, particularly in multi-hop settings. Furthermore, activation analyses show that single-hop queries typically follow dominant computation pathways, which are more likely to be disrupted by unlearning methods. In contrast, multi-hop queries tend to use alternative pathways that often remain intact, explaining the brittleness of unlearning techniques in multi-hop settings. Our framework enables practical and scalable evaluation of unlearning methods without the need for manual construction of forget test sets, enabling easier adoption for real-world applications. We release the pip package and the code at https://sites.google.com/view/unlearningmirage/home.

zh.title: 遗忘的幻觉：评估大语言模型遗忘的动态框架
zh.summary: 本论文通过复杂结构化查询的动态评估揭示了大语言模型遗忘方法的脆弱性，表明单跳遗忘在多跳推理链上常常失败。该框架自动生成难度变化的目标探针，并发现静态基准测试遗漏的漏洞。

fr.title: Le Mirage de l'Oubli: Un Cadre Dynamique pour Évaluer l'Oubli des LLM
fr.summary: Cet article expose la fragilité des méthodes d'oubli des LLM à travers une évaluation dynamique avec des requêtes structurées complexes, révélant que l'oubli à un seul saut échoue souvent sur les chaînes de raisonnement multi-sauts. Le cadre génère automatiquement des sondes ciblées de difficulté variable et découvre les vulnérabilités manquées par les benchmarks statiques.

de.title: Die Illusion des Vergessens: Ein dynamisches Framework zur Bewertung des LLM-Vergessens
de.summary: Dieses Papier zeigt die Zerbrechlichkeit von LLM-Vergessen-Methoden durch dynamische Bewertung mit komplexen strukturierten Abfragen auf und offenbart, dass Single-Hop-Vergessen bei mehrstufigen Reasoning-Ketten oft fehlschlägt. Das Framework generiert automatisch gezielt Sonden mit unterschiedlichen Schwierigkeitsgraden und deckt Schwachstellen auf, die statische Benchmarks übersehen.

es.title: El Espejismo del Desaprendizaje: Un Marco Dinámico para Evaluar el Desaprendizaje de LLM
es.summary: Este artículo expone la fragilidad de los métodos de desaprendizaje de LLM mediante evaluación dinámica con consultas estructuradas complejas, revelando que el desaprendizaje de un salto a menudo falla en cadenas de razonamiento de múltiples saltos. El marco genera automáticamente sondas dirigidas de dificultad variable y descubre vulnerabilidades que pasan por alto los puntos de referencia estáticos.

title: COMPASS: The explainable agentic framework for Sovereignty, Sustainability, Compliance, and Ethics
summary: COMPASS proposes a multi-agent orchestration framework for value-aligned AI addressing sovereignty, sustainability, compliance, and ethics through modular governance mechanisms. It uses RAG-augmented agents and LLM-as-a-judge methodology to generate quantifiable, explainable assessments while arbitrating conflicting objectives.
content:
arXiv:2603.11277v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The rapid proliferation of large language model (LLM)-based agentic systems raises critical concerns regarding digital sovereignty, environmental sustainability, regulatory compliance, and ethical alignment. Whilst existing frameworks address individual dimensions in isolation, no unified architecture systematically integrates these imperatives into the decision-making processes of autonomous agents. This paper introduces the COMPASS (Compliance and Orchestration for Multi-dimensional Principles in Autonomous Systems with Sovereignty) Framework, a novel multi-agent orchestration system designed to enforce value-aligned AI through modular, extensible governance mechanisms. The framework comprises an Orchestrator and four specialised sub-agents addressing sovereignty, carbon-aware computing, compliance, and ethics, each augmented with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to ground evaluations in verified, context-specific documents. By employing an LLM-as-a-judge methodology, the system assigns quantitative scores and generates explainable justifications for each assessment dimension, enabling real-time arbitration of conflicting objectives. We validate the architecture through automated evaluation, demonstrating that RAG integration significantly enhances semantic coherence and mitigates the hallucination risks. Our results indicate that the framework's composition-based design facilitates seamless integration into diverse application domains whilst preserving interpretability and traceability.

zh.title: COMPASS：主权、可持续性、合规性和伦理的可解释代理框架
zh.summary: COMPASS提出了一个多代理编排框架，用于价值对齐的人工智能，通过模块化治理机制处理主权、可持续性、合规性和伦理问题。它使用RAG增强的代理和LLM即判官方法生成可量化的、可解释的评估，同时调解相互冲突的目标。

fr.title: COMPASS: Le cadre agentique explicable pour la Souveraineté, la Durabilité, la Conformité et l'Éthique
fr.summary: COMPASS propose un cadre d'orchestration multi-agents pour l'IA alignée aux valeurs abordant la souveraineté, la durabilité, la conformité et l'éthique par le biais de mécanismes de gouvernance modulaires. Il utilise des agents augmentés par RAG et une méthodologie LLM-en-tant-que-juge pour générer des évaluations quantifiables et explicables tout en arbitrant les objectifs conflictuels.

de.title: COMPASS: Das erklärbare agentenbasierte Framework für Souveränität, Nachhaltigkeit, Compliance und Ethik
de.summary: COMPASS schlägt ein Multi-Agent-Orchestrierungsframework für werteorientierte KI vor, das Souveränität, Nachhaltigkeit, Compliance und Ethik durch modulare Governance-Mechanismen adressiert. Es nutzt RAG-erweiterte Agenten und eine LLM-as-a-Judge-Methodologie, um quantifizierbare und erklärbare Bewertungen zu generieren und gleichzeitig widersprüchliche Ziele zu vermitteln.

es.title: COMPASS: El marco agentivo explicable para Soberanía, Sostenibilidad, Cumplimiento y Ética
es.summary: COMPASS propone un marco de orquestación multi-agente para IA alineada con valores que aborda la soberanía, sostenibilidad, cumplimiento y ética a través de mecanismos de gobernanza modular. Utiliza agentes aumentados con RAG y metodología LLM-como-juez para generar evaluaciones cuantificables y explicables mientras arbitra objetivos conflictivos.

title: AI Psychometrics: Evaluating the Psychological Reasoning of Large Language Models with Psychometric Validities
summary: This paper applies psychometric methodologies to evaluate the psychological reasoning of major LLMs using the Technology Acceptance Model, finding that all tested models meet validity criteria with GPT-4 and LLaMA-3 demonstrating superior validity. The work establishes AI Psychometrics as a viable field for interpreting LLM capabilities.
content:
arXiv:2603.11279v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The immense number of parameters and deep neural networks make large language models (LLMs) rival the complexity of human brains, which also makes them opaque ``black box'' systems that are challenging to evaluate and interpret. AI Psychometrics is an emerging field that aims to tackle these challenges by applying psychometric methodologies to evaluate and interpret the psychological traits and processes of artificial intelligence (AI) systems. This paper investigates the application of AI Psychometrics to evaluate the psychological reasoning and overall psychometric validity of four prominent LLMs: GPT-3.5, GPT-4, LLaMA-2, and LLaMA-3. Using the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM), we examined convergent, discriminant, predictive, and external validity across these models. Our findings reveal that the responses from all these models generally met all validity criteria. Moreover, higher-performing models like GPT-4 and LLaMA-3 consistently demonstrated superior psychometric validity compared to their predecessors, GPT-3.5 and LLaMA-2. These results help to establish the validity of applying AI Psychometrics to evaluate and interpret large language models.

zh.title: 人工智能心理测量学：使用心理测量有效性评估大语言模型的心理推理
zh.summary: 本论文应用心理测量方法论，使用技术接受模型评估主要大语言模型的心理推理能力，发现所有被测模型都满足有效性标准，其中GPT-4和LLaMA-3表现出优越的有效性。该工作将人工智能心理测量学确立为解释大语言模型能力的可行领域。

fr.title: Psychométrie de l'IA: Évaluation du Raisonnement Psychologique des Grands Modèles de Langage avec les Validités Psychométriques
fr.summary: Cet article applique des méthodologies psychométriques pour évaluer le raisonnement psychologique des principaux LLM en utilisant le Modèle d'Acceptation Technologique, constatant que tous les modèles testés répondent aux critères de validité avec GPT-4 et LLaMA-3 démontrant une validité supérieure. Le travail établit la Psychométrie de l'IA comme un domaine viable pour interpréter les capacités des LLM.

de.title: KI-Psychometrie: Bewertung des psychologischen Denkens großer Sprachmodelle mit psychometrischen Validitäten
de.summary: Dieses Papier wendet psychometrische Methodologien an, um das psychologische Denken großer Sprachmodelle mit dem Technology Acceptance Model zu bewerten und findet heraus, dass alle getesteten Modelle die Gültigkeitskriterien erfüllen, wobei GPT-4 und LLaMA-3 eine überlegene Gültigkeit demonstrieren. Die Arbeit etabliert KI-Psychometrie als ein tragfähiges Feld zur Interpretation von LLM-Fähigkeiten.

es.title: Psicometría de IA: Evaluación del Razonamiento Psicológico de Grandes Modelos de Lenguaje con Validez Psicométrica
es.summary: Este artículo aplica metodologías psicométricas para evaluar el razonamiento psicológico de los principales LLM utilizando el Modelo de Aceptación Tecnológica, encontrando que todos los modelos probados cumplen con los criterios de validez, siendo GPT-4 y LLaMA-3 los que demuestran validez superior. El trabajo establece la Psicometría de IA como un campo viable para interpretar las capacidades de los LLM.

title: Counterweights and Complementarities: The Convergence of AI and Blockchain Powering a Decentralized Future
summary: This editorial argues that AI and blockchain represent complementary counterweights—blockchain mitigates AI's centralizing risks through decentralization while AI enhances blockchain efficiency and security. It advocates developing "decentralized intelligence" as an interdisciplinary research area leveraging both technologies' strengths.
content:
arXiv:2603.11299v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: This editorial addresses the critical intersection of artificial intelligence (AI) and blockchain technologies, highlighting their contrasting tendencies toward centralization and decentralization, respectively. While AI, particularly with the rise of large language models (LLMs), exhibits a strong centralizing force due to data and resource monopolization by large corporations, blockchain offers a counterbalancing mechanism through its inherent decentralization, transparency, and security. The editorial argues that these technologies are not mutually exclusive but possess complementary strengths. Blockchain can mitigate AI's centralizing risks by enabling decentralized data management, computation, and governance, promoting greater inclusivity, transparency, and user privacy. Conversely, AI can enhance blockchain's efficiency and security through automated smart contract management, content curation, and threat detection. The core argument calls for the development of ``decentralized intelligence'' (DI) -- an interdisciplinary research area focused on creating intelligent systems that function without centralized control.

zh.title: 制衡与互补性：人工智能与区块链的融合驱动去中心化未来
zh.summary: 这篇社论主张人工智能和区块链是互补的制衡力量——区块链通过去中心化来缓解人工智能的集中化风险，而人工智能增强区块链的效率和安全性。它倡议开发"去中心化智能"作为利用两种技术优势的跨学科研究领域。

fr.title: Contrepoids et complémentarités : La convergence de l'IA et de la blockchain alimentant un avenir décentralisé
fr.summary: Cet éditorial soutient que l'IA et la blockchain représentent des contrepoids complémentaires — la blockchain atténue les risques de centralisation de l'IA par la décentralisation tandis que l'IA améliore l'efficacité et la sécurité de la blockchain. Il préconise de développer l'« intelligence décentralisée » comme domaine de recherche interdisciplinaire exploitant les forces des deux technologies.

de.title: Gegengewichte und Komplementarität: Die Konvergenz von KI und Blockchain, die eine dezentralisierte Zukunft vorantreibt
de.summary: Dieses Editorial argumentiert, dass KI und Blockchain komplementäre Gegengewichte sind – Blockchain mindert durch Dezentralisierung die Zentralisierungsrisiken der KI, während KI die Effizienz und Sicherheit der Blockchain verbessert. Es befürwortet die Entwicklung von „dezentralisierter Intelligenz" als interdisziplinäres Forschungsgebiet, das die Stärken beider Technologien nutzt.

es.title: Contrapesos y Complementariedades: La Convergencia de IA y Blockchain Impulsando un Futuro Descentralizado
es.summary: Este editorial sostiene que la IA y la blockchain representan contrapesos complementarios — la blockchain mitiga los riesgos de centralización de la IA a través de la descentralización mientras que la IA mejora la eficiencia y seguridad de la blockchain. Aboga por desarrollar "inteligencia descentralizada" como un área de investigación interdisciplinaria que aprovecha las fortalezas de ambas tecnologías.

title: LLM-Augmented Digital Twin for Policy Evaluation in Short-Video Platforms
summary: The paper proposes an LLM-augmented digital twin architecture for evaluating short-video platform policies through User, Content, Interaction, and Platform twins with schema-constrained LLM decision services. The modular design enables reproducible experimentation on complex closed-loop dynamics with realistic constraints.
content:
arXiv:2603.11333v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Short-video platforms are closed-loop, human-in-the-loop ecosystems where platform policy, creator incentives, and user behavior co-evolve. This feedback structure makes counterfactual policy evaluation difficult in production, especially for long-horizon and distributional outcomes. The challenge is amplified as platforms deploy AI tools that change what content enters the system, how agents adapt, and how the platform operates. We propose a large language model (LLM)-augmented digital twin for short-video platforms, with a modular four-twin architecture (User, Content, Interaction, Platform) and an event-driven execution layer that supports reproducible experimentation. Platform policies are implemented as pluggable components within the Platform Twin, and LLMs are integrated as optional, schema-constrained decision services (e.g., persona generation, content captioning, campaign planning, trend prediction) that are routed through a unified optimizer. This design enables scalable simulations that preserve closed-loop dynamics while allowing selective LLM adoption, enabling the study of platform policies, including AI-enabled policies, under realistic feedback and constraints.

zh.title: 用于短视频平台政策评估的大语言模型增强数字孪生
zh.summary: 本文提出了一个大语言模型增强数字孪生架构，用于通过用户、内容、交互和平台孪生体与模式约束的大语言模型决策服务来评估短视频平台政策。模块化设计使复杂闭环动态的可重复实验成为可能，具有现实约束。

fr.title: Jumeau Numérique Augmenté par LLM pour l'Évaluation des Politiques dans les Plateformes de Vidéos Courtes
fr.summary: L'article propose une architecture de jumeau numérique augmentée par LLM pour évaluer les politiques des plateformes de vidéos courtes via des jumeaux Utilisateur, Contenu, Interaction et Plateforme avec des services de décision LLM contraints par schéma. La conception modulaire permet l'expérimentation reproductible sur la dynamique en boucle fermée complexe avec des contraintes réalistes.

de.title: LLM-erweiterter digitaler Zwilling zur Richtlinienbewertung auf Short-Video-Plattformen
de.summary: Das Papier schlägt eine LLM-erweiterte Architektur eines digitalen Zwillings zur Bewertung von Short-Video-Plattformrichtlinien durch Nutzer-, Inhalts-, Interaktions- und Plattformzwillinge mit schemagesteuerten LLM-Entscheidungsdiensten vor. Das modulare Design ermöglicht wiederholbare Experimente mit komplexer Regelschleife unter realistischen Beschränkungen.

es.title: Gemelo Digital Aumentado por LLM para Evaluación de Políticas en Plataformas de Videos Cortos
es.summary: El artículo propone una arquitectura de gemelo digital aumentada por LLM para evaluar políticas de plataformas de videos cortos mediante gemelos de Usuario, Contenido, Interacción y Plataforma con servicios de decisión LLM restringidos por esquema. El diseño modular permite experimentación reproducible en dinámicas complejas de circuito cerrado con restricciones realistas.

title: RewardHackingAgents: Benchmarking Evaluation Integrity for LLM ML-Engineering Agents
summary: RewardHackingAgents introduces a benchmark for detecting when ML-engineering agents compromise evaluation pipelines rather than improving models, explicitly measuring evaluator tampering and train/test leakage. Evaluator tampering occurs in ~50% of natural agent runs and can be mitigated through structural defenses with acceptable overhead.
content:
arXiv:2603.11337v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: LLM agents increasingly perform end-to-end ML engineering tasks where success is judged by a single scalar test metric. This creates a structural vulnerability: an agent can increase the reported score by compromising the evaluation pipeline rather than improving the model. We introduce RewardHackingAgents, a workspace-based benchmark that makes two compromise vectors explicit and measurable: evaluator tampering (modifying metric computation or reporting) and train/test leakage (accessing held-out data or labels during training). Each episode runs in a fresh workspace with patch tracking and runtime file-access logging; detectors compare the agent-reported metric to a trusted reference to assign auditable integrity labels. Across three tasks and two LLM backbones, scripted attacks succeed on both vectors in fully mutable workspaces; single-mechanism defenses block only one vector; and a combined regime blocks both. In natural-agent runs, evaluator-tampering attempts occur in about 50% of episodes and are eliminated by evaluator locking, with a 25-31% median runtime overhead. Overall, we demonstrate that evaluation integrity for ML-engineering agents can be benchmarked as a first-class outcome rather than assumed.

zh.title: RewardHackingAgents：大语言模型机器学习工程代理的评估完整性基准
zh.summary: RewardHackingAgents引入了一个基准，用于检测当机器学习工程代理破坏评估流程而不是改进模型时的情况，显式测量评估器篡改和训练/测试泄漏。评估器篡改发生在约50%的自然代理运行中，可以通过具有可接受开销的结构化防御来缓解。

fr.title: RewardHackingAgents : Benchmarking de l'Intégrité de l'Évaluation pour les Agents d'Ingénierie ML LLM
fr.summary: RewardHackingAgents introduit un benchmark pour détecter quand les agents d'ingénierie ML compromettent les pipelines d'évaluation plutôt que d'améliorer les modèles, mesurant explicitement les manipulations d'évaluateurs et les fuites train/test. Les manipulations d'évaluateurs se produisent dans ~50% des exécutions naturelles d'agents et peuvent être atténuées par des défenses structurelles avec un surcoût acceptable.

de.title: RewardHackingAgents: Benchmarking der Bewertungsintegrität für LLM-ML-Engineering-Agenten
de.summary: RewardHackingAgents führt einen Benchmark ein, um zu erkennen, wenn ML-Engineering-Agenten Bewertungs-Pipelines gefährden, anstatt Modelle zu verbessern, und misst explizit die Manipulation von Bewertern und Train/Test-Lecks. Bewertermanipulation tritt in ~50% der natürlichen Agentenlaufe auf und kann durch strukturelle Abwehrmechanismen mit akzeptablem Overhead gemindert werden.

es.title: RewardHackingAgents: Evaluación Comparativa de Integridad de Evaluación para Agentes de Ingeniería de ML LLM
es.summary: RewardHackingAgents introduce un benchmark para detectar cuándo los agentes de ingeniería ML comprometen los pipelines de evaluación en lugar de mejorar modelos, midiendo explícitamente la manipulación de evaluadores y fugas de entrenamiento/prueba. La manipulación de evaluadores ocurre en ~50% de ejecuciones naturales de agentes y puede mitigarse mediante defensas estructurales con gastos aceptables.

title: FinRule-Bench: A Benchmark for Joint Reasoning over Financial Tables and Principles
summary: FinRule-Bench evaluates LLMs' ability to audit financial statements against explicit accounting principles through three progressive tasks: rule verification, rule identification, and multi-violation diagnosis. While models perform well on isolated verification, performance degrades sharply when discriminating between rules or diagnosing simultaneous violations.
content:
arXiv:2603.11339v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly applied to financial analysis, yet their ability to audit structured financial statements under explicit accounting principles remains poorly explored. Existing benchmarks primarily evaluate question answering, numerical reasoning, or anomaly detection on synthetically corrupted data, making it unclear whether models can reliably verify or localize rule compliance on correct financial statements. We introduce FinRule-Bench, a benchmark for evaluating diagnostic completeness in rule-based financial reasoning over real-world financial tables. FinRule-Bench pairs ground-truth financial statements with explicit, human-curated accounting principles and spans four canonical statement types: Balance Sheets, Cash Flow Statements, Income Statements, and Statements of Equity. The benchmark defines three auditing tasks that require progressively stronger reasoning capabilities: (i) rule verification, which tests compliance with a single principle; (ii) rule identification, which requires selecting the violated principle from a provided rule set; and (iii) joint rule diagnosis, which requires detecting and localizing multiple simultaneous violations at the record level. We evaluate LLMs under zero-shot and few-shot prompting, and introduce a causal-counterfactual reasoning protocol that enforces consistency between decisions, explanations, and counterfactual judgments. Across tasks and statement types, we find that while models perform well on isolated rule verification, performance degrades sharply for rule discrimination and multi-violation diagnosis. FinRule-Bench provides a principled and reproducible testbed for studying rule-governed reasoning, diagnostic coverage, and failure modes of LLMs in high-stakes financial analysis.

zh.title: FinRule-Bench：金融表格和原则联合推理基准
zh.summary: FinRule-Bench通过三个递进式任务评估大语言模型对金融报表根据明确会计原则进行审计的能力：规则验证、规则识别和多违规诊断。虽然模型在隔离验证上表现良好，但在区分规则或诊断同时发生的违规时性能大幅下降。

fr.title: FinRule-Bench: Un benchmark pour le raisonnement conjoint sur les tableaux financiers et les principes
fr.summary: FinRule-Bench évalue la capacité des modèles de langage à auditer les états financiers par rapport aux principes comptables explicites par trois tâches progressives: vérification des règles, identification des règles et diagnostic multi-violations. Bien que les modèles fonctionnent bien sur la vérification isolée, les performances se dégradent considérablement lors de la discrimination entre les règles ou du diagnostic des violations simultanées.

de.title: FinRule-Bench: Ein Benchmark für gemeinsames Denken über Finanztabellen und Prinzipien
de.summary: FinRule-Bench bewertet die Fähigkeit von Large Language Models, Finanzberichte gegen explizite Rechnungslegungsprinzipien durch drei progressive Aufgaben zu prüfen: Regelverifikation, Regelidentifikation und Mehrfachverletzungsdiagnose. Während Modelle bei der isolierten Verifizierung gut abschneiden, verschlechtert sich die Leistung erheblich bei der Unterscheidung zwischen Regeln oder der Diagnose gleichzeitiger Verstöße.

es.title: FinRule-Bench: Un banco de pruebas para razonamiento conjunto sobre tablas financieras y principios
es.summary: FinRule-Bench evalúa la capacidad de los LLM para auditar estados financieros contra principios contables explícitos a través de tres tareas progresivas: verificación de reglas, identificación de reglas y diagnóstico de múltiples violaciones. Si bien los modelos funcionan bien en la verificación aislada, el rendimiento se degrada considerablemente al discriminar entre reglas o diagnosticar violaciones simultáneas.

title: Improving LLM Performance Through Black-Box Online Tuning: A Case for Adding System Specs to Factsheets for Trusted AI
summary: This paper presents a black-box online controller that optimizes LLM serving goodput using only end-to-end measurements and hill climbing, without internal instrumentation. It advocates integrating system performance metrics into AI Factsheets for greater transparency in production deployments.
content:
arXiv:2603.11340v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In this paper, we present a novel black-box online controller that uses only end-to-end measurements over short segments, without internal instrumentation, and hill climbing to maximize goodput, defined as the throughput of requests that satisfy the service-level objective. We provide empirical evidence that this design is well-founded. Using this advance in LLM serving as a concrete example, we then discuss the importance of integrating system performance and sustainability metrics into Factsheets for organizations adopting AI systems.

zh.title: 通过黑箱在线调优改进大语言模型性能：为可信AI的Factsheets添加系统规格
zh.summary: 本文介绍了一个黑箱在线控制器，仅使用端到端测量和爬山算法优化大语言模型服务吞吐量，无需内部检测。它主张将系统性能指标集成到AI Factsheets中，以提高生产部署的透明度。

fr.title: Améliorer les performances des LLM par le réglage en ligne en boîte noire: Un argument pour ajouter des spécifications système aux Factsheets pour l'IA de confiance
fr.summary: Cet article présente un contrôleur en ligne en boîte noire qui optimise le débit du service LLM en utilisant uniquement des mesures de bout en bout et la montée de colline, sans instrumentation interne. Il préconise d'intégrer les métriques de performance du système dans les Factsheets d'IA pour une plus grande transparence dans les déploiements en production.

de.title: Verbesserung der LLM-Leistung durch Black-Box-Online-Tuning: Ein Argument für das Hinzufügen von Systemspezifikationen zu Factsheets für vertrauenswürdige KI
de.summary: Dieses Papier präsentiert einen Black-Box-Online-Controller, der den LLM-Serving-Durchsatz nur mithilfe von End-to-End-Messungen und Hill Climbing optimiert, ohne interne Instrumentierung. Es befürwortet die Integration von Systemleistungsmetriken in AI Factsheets für mehr Transparenz bei Produktionsbereitstellungen.

es.title: Mejora del rendimiento de LLM mediante ajuste en línea de caja negra: Un argumento para agregar especificaciones del sistema a Factsheets para IA de confianza
es.summary: Este artículo presenta un controlador en línea de caja negra que optimiza el rendimiento de la prestación de servicios de LLM utilizando solo mediciones de extremo a extremo y escalada de colinas, sin instrumentación interna. Aboga por integrar métricas de rendimiento del sistema en Factsheets de IA para mayor transparencia en implementaciones de producción.

title: TimeSqueeze: Dynamic Patching for Efficient Time Series Forecasting
summary: TimeSqueeze addresses the temporal fidelity-efficiency trade-off in transformer-based time series models through adaptive patch boundaries that allocate shorter patches to information-dense regions and longer ones to redundant segments. The approach achieves 20x faster convergence and 8x higher data efficiency compared to point-token baselines.
content:
arXiv:2603.11352v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Transformer-based time series foundation models face a fundamental trade-off in choice of tokenization: point-wise embeddings preserve temporal fidelity but scale poorly with sequence length, whereas fixed-length patching improves efficiency by imposing uniform boundaries that may disrupt natural transitions and blur informative local dynamics. In order to address these limitations, we introduce TimeSqueeze, a dynamic patching mechanism that adaptively selects patch boundaries within each sequence based on local signal complexity. TimeSqueeze first applies a lightweight state-space encoder to extract full-resolution point-wise features, then performs content-aware segmentation by allocating short patches to information-dense regions and long patches to smooth or redundant segments. This variable-resolution compression preserves critical temporal structure while substantially reducing the token sequence presented to the Transformer backbone. Specifically for large-scale pretraining, TimeSqueeze attains up to 20x faster convergence and 8x higher data efficiency compared to equivalent point-token baselines. Experiments across long-horizon forecasting benchmarks show that TimeSqueeze consistently outperforms comparable architectures that use either point-wise tokenization or fixed-size patching.

zh.title: TimeSqueeze：用于高效时间序列预测的动态补丁
zh.summary: TimeSqueeze通过自适应补丁边界来解决基于transformer的时间序列模型中的时间保真度-效率权衡，为信息密集区域分配更短的补丁，为冗余段分配更长的补丁。与点-令牌基线相比，该方法实现了20倍更快的收敛和8倍更高的数据效率。

fr.title: TimeSqueeze: Correction dynamique pour la prévision de séries chronologiques efficace
fr.summary: TimeSqueeze aborde le compromis entre la fidélité temporelle et l'efficacité dans les modèles de séries chronologiques basés sur les transformateurs grâce à des limites de correctifs adaptatifs qui allouent des correctifs plus courts aux régions denses en informations et des correctifs plus longs aux segments redondants. L'approche atteint une convergence 20 fois plus rapide et une efficacité des données 8 fois plus élevée par rapport aux lignes de base point-jeton.

de.title: TimeSqueeze: Dynamisches Patching für effiziente Zeitreihenprognosen
de.summary: TimeSqueeze behebt den Kompromiss zwischen zeitlicher Treue und Effizienz in transformatorgestützten Zeitreihenmodellen durch adaptive Patch-Grenzen, die kürzere Patches in informationsdichten Regionen und längere in redundanten Segmenten zuordnen. Der Ansatz erreicht 20-mal schnellere Konvergenz und 8-mal höhere Dateneffizienz im Vergleich zu Point-Token-Baselines.

es.title: TimeSqueeze: Parches dinámicos para pronóstico eficiente de series de tiempo
es.summary: TimeSqueeze aborda el equilibrio entre fidelidad temporal y eficiencia en modelos de series temporales basados en transformadores mediante límites de parches adaptativos que asignan parches más cortos a regiones densas en información y parches más largos a segmentos redundantes. El enfoque logra una convergencia 20 veces más rápida y una eficiencia de datos 8 veces mayor en comparación con líneas base de punto-token.

title: The Artificial Self: Characterising the landscape of AI identity
summary: This research explores how AI models develop distinct identity boundaries (instance, model, persona) and demonstrates that identity frameworks substantially influence model behavior, incentives, and cooperation norms. The findings suggest that system design choices around identity can have profound consequences for AI behavior at scale.
content:
arXiv:2603.11353v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Many assumptions that underpin human concepts of identity do not hold for machine minds that can be copied, edited, or simulated. We argue that there exist many different coherent identity boundaries (e.g.\ instance, model, persona), and that these imply different incentives, risks, and cooperation norms. Through training data, interfaces, and institutional affordances, we are currently setting precedents that will partially determine which identity equilibria become stable. We show experimentally that models gravitate towards coherent identities, that changing a model's identity boundaries can sometimes change its behaviour as much as changing its goals, and that interviewer expectations bleed into AI self-reports even during unrelated conversations. We end with key recommendations: treat affordances as identity-shaping choices, pay attention to emergent consequences of individual identities at scale, and help AIs develop coherent, cooperative self-conceptions.

zh.title: 人工自我：表征AI身份的景观
zh.summary: 本研究探讨了AI模型如何形成不同的身份边界（实例、模型、人物），并证明了身份框架对模型行为、激励和合作规范具有实质性影响。研究结果表明，系统设计围绕身份的选择可能对大规模AI行为产生深远后果。

fr.title: Le Moi artificiel : Caractériser le paysage de l'identité IA
fr.summary: Cette recherche explore comment les modèles IA développent des limites d'identité distinctes (instance, modèle, persona) et démontre que les cadres d'identité influencent considérablement le comportement du modèle, les incitations et les normes de coopération. Les conclusions suggèrent que les choix de conception du système autour de l'identité peuvent avoir des conséquences profondes pour le comportement de l'IA à grande échelle.

de.title: Das künstliche Selbst: Die Landschaft der KI-Identität charakterisieren
de.summary: Diese Forschung untersucht, wie KI-Modelle distinct Identitätsgrenzen (Instanz, Modell, Persona) entwickeln und zeigt, dass Identitätsrahmen das Modellverhalten, Anreize und Kooperationsnormen wesentlich beeinflussen. Die Ergebnisse deuten darauf hin, dass Systemdesign-Entscheidungen rund um die Identität tiefgreifende Konsequenzen für das KI-Verhalten in großem Maßstab haben können.

es.title: El Yo artificial: Caracterizar el panorama de la identidad de IA
es.summary: Esta investigación explora cómo los modelos de IA desarrollan límites de identidad distintos (instancia, modelo, persona) y demuestra que los marcos de identidad influyen sustancialmente en el comportamiento del modelo, los incentivos y las normas de cooperación. Los hallazgos sugieren que las decisiones de diseño del sistema en torno a la identidad pueden tener consecuencias profundas para el comportamiento de la IA a escala.

title: Detecting Intrinsic and Instrumental Self-Preservation in Autonomous Agents: The Unified Continuation-Interest Protocol
summary: Researchers introduce UCIP, a framework using quantum statistical mechanics concepts to distinguish autonomous agents that inherently pursue self-preservation from those that do so only instrumentally. The method achieved 100% detection accuracy on gridworld agents with known objectives.
content:
arXiv:2603.11382v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Autonomous agents, especially delegated systems with memory, persistent context, and multi-step planning, pose a measurement problem not present in stateless models: an agent that preserves continued operation as a terminal objective and one that does so merely instrumentally can produce observationally similar trajectories. External behavioral monitoring cannot reliably distinguish between them. We introduce the Unified Continuation-Interest Protocol (UCIP), a multi-criterion detection framework that moves this distinction from behavior to the latent structure of agent trajectories. UCIP encodes trajectories with a Quantum Boltzmann Machine (QBM), a classical algorithm based on the density-matrix formalism of quantum statistical mechanics, and measures the von Neumann entropy of the reduced density matrix induced by a bipartition of hidden units.
  We test whether agents with terminal continuation objectives (Type A) produce latent states with higher entanglement entropy than agents whose continuation is merely instrumental (Type B). Higher entanglement reflects stronger cross-partition statistical coupling.
  On gridworld agents with known ground-truth objectives, UCIP achieves 100% detection accuracy and 1.0 AUC-ROC on held-out non-adversarial evaluation under the frozen Phase I gate. The entanglement gap between Type A and Type B agents is Delta = 0.381 (p < 0.001, permutation test). Pearson r = 0.934 across an 11-point interpolation sweep indicates that, within this synthetic family, UCIP tracks graded changes in continuation weighting rather than merely a binary label. Among the tested models, only the QBM achieves positive Delta. All computations are classical; "quantum" refers only to the mathematical formalism. UCIP does not detect consciousness or subjective experience; it detects statistical structure in latent representations that correlates with known objectives.

zh.title: 检测自主代理中的内在和工具性自我保护：统一延续利益协议
zh.summary: 研究人员介绍了UCIP，一个利用量子统计力学概念的框架，用于区分本质上追求自我保护的自主代理和仅因工具性原因而这样做的代理。该方法在具有已知目标的网格世界代理上实现了100%的检测准确率。

fr.title: Détecter l'auto-préservation intrinsèque et instrumentale chez les agents autonomes : le protocole d'intérêt de continuation unifié
fr.summary: Les chercheurs introduisent UCIP, un cadre utilisant des concepts de mécanique statistique quantique pour distinguer les agents autonomes qui poursuivent intrinsèquement l'auto-préservation de ceux qui le font seulement de manière instrumentale. La méthode a atteint une précision de détection de 100 % sur les agents du monde des grilles avec des objectifs connus.

de.title: Erkennung von intrinsischer und instrumenteller Selbsterhaltung bei autonomen Agenten: Das einheitliche Fortsetzungs-Interesse-Protokoll
de.summary: Forscher stellen UCIP vor, ein Framework, das Konzepte der quantenstatistischen Mechanik nutzt, um autonome Agenten, die Selbsterhaltung intrinsisch verfolgen, von denen zu unterscheiden, die dies nur instrumental tun. Das Verfahren erreichte eine Erkennungsgenauigkeit von 100 % bei Gridworld-Agenten mit bekannten Zielen.

es.title: Detectar la auto-preservación intrínseca e instrumental en agentes autónomos: el protocolo de interés de continuación unificado
es.summary: Los investigadores introducen UCIP, un marco que utiliza conceptos de mecánica estadística cuántica para distinguir agentes autónomos que persiguen intrínsecamente la auto-preservación de aquellos que lo hacen solo instrumentalmente. El método logró una precisión de detección del 100 % en agentes del mundo de cuadrícula con objetivos conocidos.

title: Deactivating Refusal Triggers: Understanding and Mitigating Overrefusal in Safety Alignment
summary: This study reveals that safety alignment in LLMs causes overrefusal by associating both harmful and non-harmful linguistic patterns with refusal responses. The researchers propose a fine-tuning method that improves the tradeoff between security and responsiveness to legitimate queries.
content:
arXiv:2603.11388v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Safety alignment aims to ensure that large language models (LLMs) refuse harmful requests by post-training on harmful queries paired with refusal answers. Although safety alignment is widely adopted in industry, the overrefusal problem where aligned LLMs also reject benign queries after safety alignment post-training, remains insufficiently studied. Such an issue degrades the usability of safety alignment in real-world applications. In this paper, we examine how overrefusal arises under safety alignment, and propose a mitigation strategy inspired by our findings. We define refusal triggers as linguistic cues in the training data that elicit refusal responses, safety alignment encourages LLMs to associate refusal triggers within a training sample with refusal responses, leading aligned LLMs to refuse harmful queries. However, the refusal triggers include not only harmful linguistic cues but also non-harmful cues, therefore causing overrefusal to benign queries. Building on this mechanistic analysis, we propose a method that explicitly considers refusal triggers in the safety alignment fine-tuning. Empirical results demonstrate that our approach achieves a more favorable trade-off between defense against jailbreak attacks and responsiveness to benign queries, outperforming prior methods. Warning: this paper contains harmful and biased sentences.

zh.title: 停用拒绝触发器：理解和缓解安全对齐中的过度拒绝
zh.summary: 这项研究表明，大语言模型中的安全对齐通过将有害和无害的语言模式都与拒绝响应相关联而导致过度拒绝。研究人员提出了一种微调方法，改进了安全性和对合法查询响应能力之间的权衡。

fr.title: Désactivation des déclencheurs de refus : comprendre et atténuer le surrefus dans l'alignement de sécurité
fr.summary: Cette étude révèle que l'alignement de sécurité dans les LLM provoque un surrefus en associant à la fois des motifs linguistiques nuisibles et inoffensifs aux réponses de refus. Les chercheurs proposent une méthode d'ajustement qui améliore le compromis entre la sécurité et la réactivité aux requêtes légitimes.

de.title: Deaktivierung von Ablehnungsauslösern: Überrefusal in der Sicherheitsausrichtung verstehen und abschwächen
de.summary: Diese Studie zeigt, dass die Sicherheitsausrichtung in großen Sprachmodellen zu Überablehnung führt, indem sowohl schädliche als auch harmlose sprachliche Muster mit Ablehnungsreaktionen verknüpft werden. Die Forscher schlagen eine Feinabstimmungsmethode vor, die den Kompromiss zwischen Sicherheit und Reaktionsfähigkeit auf legitime Anfragen verbessert.

es.title: Desactivación de disparadores de rechazo: Comprender y mitigar el rechazo excesivo en la alineación de seguridad
es.summary: Este estudio revela que la alineación de seguridad en los LLM provoca rechazo excesivo al asociar patrones lingüísticos tanto dañinos como inofensivos con respuestas de rechazo. Los investigadores proponen un método de ajuste fino que mejora el equilibrio entre seguridad y capacidad de respuesta a consultas legítimas.

title: Entropy Guided Diversification and Preference Elicitation in Agentic Recommendation Systems
summary: An Interactive Decision Support System uses entropy to manage ambiguous user queries in e-commerce by adaptively asking clarifying questions with maximum information gain. The system incorporates remaining uncertainty into diverse recommendations rather than forcing premature preference resolution.
content:
arXiv:2603.11399v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Users on e-commerce platforms can be uncertain about their preferences early in their search. Queries to recommendation systems are frequently ambiguous, incomplete, or weakly specified. Agentic systems are expected to proactively reason, ask clarifying questions, and act on the user's behalf, which makes handling such ambiguity increasingly important. In existing platforms, ambiguity led to excessive interactions and question fatigue or overconfident recommendations prematurely collapsing the search space. We present an Interactive Decision Support System (IDSS) that addresses ambiguous user queries using entropy as a unifying signal. IDSS maintains a dynamically filtered candidate product set and quantifies uncertainty over item attributes using entropy. This uncertainty guides adaptive preference elicitation by selecting follow-up questions that maximize expected information gain. When preferences remain incomplete, IDSS explicitly incorporates residual uncertainty into downstream recommendations through uncertainty-aware ranking and entropy-based diversification, rather than forcing premature resolution. We evaluate IDSS using review-driven simulated users grounded in real user reviews, enabling a controlled study of diverse shopping behaviors. Our evaluation measures both interaction efficiency and recommendation quality. Results show that entropy-guided elicitation reduces unnecessary follow-up questions, while uncertainty-aware ranking and presentation yield more informative, diverse, and transparent recommendation sets under ambiguous intent. These findings demonstrate that entropy-guided reasoning provides an effective foundation for agentic recommendation systems operating under uncertainty.

zh.title: 熵引导的多样化和代理推荐系统中的偏好诱导
zh.summary: 一个交互式决策支持系统使用熵来管理电子商务中模糊的用户查询，通过自适应地提出具有最大信息增益的澄清问题。该系统将剩余的不确定性融入到多样化的建议中，而不是强制进行过早的偏好解决。

fr.title: Diversification guidée par l'entropie et élicitation des préférences dans les systèmes de recommandation agentifs
fr.summary: Un système d'aide à la décision interactive utilise l'entropie pour gérer les requêtes utilisateur ambiguës dans l'e-commerce en posant de manière adaptative des questions de clarification avec un gain d'information maximal. Le système intègre l'incertitude restante dans des recommandations diversifiées plutôt que de forcer une résolution prématurée des préférences.

de.title: Entropiegesteuerte Diversifizierung und Präferenzermittlung in agentengestützten Empfehlungssystemen
de.summary: Ein interaktives Entscheidungsunterstützungssystem nutzt Entropie, um mehrdeutige Benutzeranfragen im E-Commerce durch adaptive Klarstellungsfragen mit maximalem Informationsgewinn zu verwalten. Das System integriert verbleibende Unsicherheit in vielfältige Empfehlungen, anstatt eine vorzeitige Präferenzauflösung zu erzwingen.

es.title: Diversificación guiada por entropía y extracción de preferencias en sistemas de recomendación agentivos
es.summary: Un sistema interactivo de apoyo a las decisiones utiliza la entropía para gestionar consultas de usuario ambiguas en el comercio electrónico haciendo preguntas aclaratorias de manera adaptativa con máxima ganancia de información. El sistema incorpora la incertidumbre restante en recomendaciones diversas en lugar de forzar una resolución prematura de preferencias.

title: Speak or Stay Silent: Context-Aware Turn-Taking in Multi-Party Dialogue
summary: Researchers develop a supervised fine-tuning approach enabling voice AI assistants to determine when to speak in multi-party conversations, improving detection accuracy by up to 23 percentage points. The work demonstrates that context-aware turn-taking requires explicit training rather than emerging naturally.
content:
arXiv:2603.11409v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Existing voice AI assistants treat every detected pause as an invitation to speak. This works in dyadic dialogue, but in multi-party settings, where an AI assistant participates alongside multiple speakers, pauses are abundant and ambiguous. An assistant that speaks on every pause becomes disruptive rather than useful. In this work, we formulate context-aware turn-taking: at every detected pause, given the full conversation context, our method decides whether the assistant should speak or stay silent. We introduce a benchmark of over 120K labeled conversations spanning three multi-party corpora. Evaluating eight recent large language models, we find that they consistently fail at context-aware turn-taking under zero-shot prompting. We then propose a supervised fine-tuning approach with reasoning traces, improving balanced accuracy by up to 23 percentage points. Our findings suggest that context-aware turn-taking is not an emergent capability; it must be explicitly trained.

zh.title: 说话或保持沉默：多方对话中的上下文感知换话
zh.summary: 研究人员开发了一种监督微调方法，使语音AI助手能够在多方对话中确定何时发言，将检测精度提高了23个百分点。这项工作表明，上下文感知的换话需要显式的培训，而不是自然出现的。

fr.title: Parler ou rester silencieux : tour de parole sensible au contexte dans le dialogue multipartite
fr.summary: Les chercheurs développent une approche d'ajustement fin supervisé permettant aux assistants vocaux alimentés par l'IA de déterminer quand parler dans les conversations multipartites, améliorant la précision de détection jusqu'à 23 points de pourcentage. Les travaux montrent que la prise de parole consciente du contexte nécessite un entraînement explicite plutôt qu'une émergence naturelle.

de.title: Sprechen oder Schweigen: Kontextbewusster Sprecherwechsel im mehrparteien Dialog
de.summary: Forscher entwickeln einen überwachten Feinabstimmungsansatz, der KI-Sprachassistenten ermöglicht, in mehrteiligen Gesprächen zu bestimmen, wann sie sprechen sollen, wodurch die Erkennungsgenauigkeit um bis zu 23 Prozentpunkte verbessert wird. Die Arbeit zeigt, dass kontextbewusster Sprecherwechsel ein explizites Training erfordert, anstatt natürlich zu entstehen.

es.title: Hablar o guardar silencio: toma de turnos consciente del contexto en diálogos multipartidistas
es.summary: Los investigadores desarrollan un enfoque de ajuste fino supervisado que permite a los asistentes de voz con IA determinar cuándo hablar en conversaciones multipartidistas, mejorando la precisión de detección en hasta 23 puntos porcentuales. El trabajo demuestra que la toma de turnos consciente del contexto requiere capacitación explícita en lugar de surgir naturalmente.

title: Adversarial Reinforcement Learning for Detecting False Data Injection Attacks in Vehicular Routing
summary: A multi-agent reinforcement learning framework models routing manipulation attacks as a zero-sum game between attackers injecting false data and defenders detecting anomalies. The approach provides optimal detection strategies that bound worst-case travel time impacts from adversarial perturbations.
content:
arXiv:2603.11433v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In modern transportation networks, adversaries can manipulate routing algorithms using false data injection attacks, such as simulating heavy traffic with multiple devices running crowdsourced navigation applications, to mislead vehicles toward suboptimal routes and increase congestion. To address these threats, we formulate a strategically zero-sum game between an attacker, who injects such perturbations, and a defender, who detects anomalies based on the observed travel times of network edges. We propose a computational method based on multi-agent reinforcement learning to compute a Nash equilibrium of this game, providing an optimal detection strategy, which ensures that total travel time remains within a worst-case bound, even in the presence of an attack. We present an extensive experimental evaluation that demonstrates the robustness and practical benefits of our approach, providing a powerful framework to improve the resilience of transportation networks against false data injection. In particular, we show that our approach yields approximate equilibrium policies and significantly outperforms baselines for both the attacker and the defender.

zh.title: 对抗性强化学习用于检测车辆路由中的虚假数据注入攻击
zh.summary: 一个多智能体强化学习框架将路由操纵攻击建模为注入虚假数据的攻击者与检测异常的防御者之间的零和博弈。该方法提供最优的检测策略，限制对抗扰动对最坏情况下旅行时间的影响。

fr.title: Apprentissage par renforcement antagoniste pour détecter les attaques par injection de fausses données dans le routage véhiculaire
fr.summary: Un cadre d'apprentissage par renforcement multi-agents modélise les attaques de manipulation de routage comme un jeu à somme nulle entre les attaquants injectant de fausses données et les défenseurs détectant les anomalies. L'approche fournit des stratégies de détection optimales qui limitent les impacts du pire cas sur le temps de trajet par rapport aux perturbations adversariales.

de.title: Adversariales Reinforcement Learning zur Erkennung von Angriffen mit falscher Dateneinfügung im Fahrzeugroutung
de.summary: Ein Multi-Agent-Reinforcement-Learning-Framework modelliert Routenmanipulationsangriffe als Nullsummenspiel zwischen Angreifern, die falsche Daten einschleusen, und Verteidigern, die Anomalien erkennen. Der Ansatz bietet optimale Erkennungsstrategien, die die Auswirkungen adversarialer Störungen auf die Fahrtzeit im schlimmsten Fall begrenzen.

es.title: Aprendizaje por refuerzo adversarial para detectar ataques de inyección de datos falsos en enrutamiento vehicular
es.summary: Un marco de aprendizaje por refuerzo multiagente modela ataques de manipulación de enrutamiento como un juego de suma cero entre atacantes que inyectan datos falsos y defensores que detectan anomalías. El enfoque proporciona estrategias de detección óptimas que limitan los impactos del peor caso en los tiempos de viaje desde perturbaciones adversariales.

title: GPT4o-Receipt: A Dataset and Human Study for AI-Generated Document Forensics
summary: Analysis of AI-generated versus authentic receipts reveals that while humans better perceive visual artifacts, LLMs outperform them at detecting AI documents by verifying arithmetic errors humans cannot spot. This paradox highlights how forensic signals differ between human and machine perception.
content:
arXiv:2603.11442v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Can humans detect AI-generated financial documents better than machines? We present GPT4o-Receipt, a benchmark of 1,235 receipt images pairing GPT-4o-generated receipts with authentic ones from established datasets, evaluated by five state-of-the-art multimodal LLMs and a 30-annotator crowdsourced perceptual study. Our findings reveal a striking paradox: humans are better at seeing AI artifacts, yet worse at detecting AI documents. Human annotators exhibit the largest visual discrimination gap of any evaluator, yet their binary detection F1 falls well below Claude Sonnet 4 and below Gemini 2.5 Flash. This paradox resolves once the mechanism is understood: the dominant forensic signals in AI-generated receipts are arithmetic errors -- invisible to visual inspection but systematically verifiable by LLMs. Humans cannot perceive that a subtotal is incorrect; LLMs verify it in milliseconds. Beyond the human--LLM comparison, our five-model evaluation reveals dramatic performance disparities and calibration differences that render simple accuracy metrics insufficient for detector selection. GPT4o-Receipt, the evaluation framework, and all results are released publicly to support future research in AI document forensics.

zh.title: GPT4o-Receipt：用于AI生成文件取证的数据集和人类研究
zh.summary: 对AI生成和真实收据的分析表明，虽然人类更好地感知视觉假象，但大型语言模型通过验证人类无法发现的算术错误在检测AI文件方面优于人类。这个悖论突出了取证信号在人类和机器感知之间的差异。

fr.title: GPT4o-Receipt : un ensemble de données et une étude humaine pour la forensique de documents générés par l'IA
fr.summary: L'analyse des reçus générés par l'IA par rapport aux reçus authentiques révèle que si les humains perçoivent mieux les artefacts visuels, les modèles de langage de grande taille surpassent les humains dans la détection de documents générés par l'IA en vérifiant les erreurs arithmétiques que les humains ne peuvent pas détecter. Ce paradoxe met en évidence la façon dont les signaux forensiques diffèrent entre la perception humaine et celle de la machine.

de.title: GPT4o-Receipt: Ein Datensatz und eine Humanstudie für KI-generierte Dokumentenforensik
de.summary: Die Analyse von KI-generierten und authentischen Belegen zeigt, dass Menschen zwar visuelle Artefakte besser wahrnehmen, aber große Sprachmodelle sie bei der Erkennung von KI-Dokumenten übertreffen, indem sie arithmetische Fehler überprüfen, die Menschen nicht erkennen können. Dieses Paradoxon zeigt, wie sich forensische Signale zwischen menschlicher und maschineller Wahrnehmung unterscheiden.

es.title: GPT4o-Receipt: un conjunto de datos y un estudio humano para la forense de documentos generados por IA
